📌 [...] they can actually use. Not a 3,000-word article. My wife handled a lot of the design and templates — she has a great eye for what actually feels useful and I focused on the build. It's been a fun project to work on together. It's completely free. No sign-up needed. You can go to checklistfox.com right now and have a checklist in 30 seconds. I'd love to hear what you think and tell me what template you wish existed. We read every comment. 🦊 — Usama & Tayyaba [...] Hey Product Hunt! 👋 I'm Usama, and I built ChecklistFox for my wife. It started from a real frustration in our own home. When we were preparing for an important trip, we were jumping between ten different websites, copy-pasting things into Google Docs, and still felt like we were missing something. It was chaos. And for moments that actually matter in life, it really shouldn't be that hard. So we built ChecklistFox - type what you're planning, get a beautiful checklist, download it as a PDF. That's genuinely it. What surprised us after launching was how many people were using it for things we never even expected - baby showers, new job prep, visa applications, weekend trips with kids. People just want a simple, clean checklist they can actually use. Not a 3,000-word article. My wife

results.title: PDFgear Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

11 views 1yr ago

Image 11: Stan

Stan

•1 review

Found PDFgear today. It is a real free pdf editor unlike 98% of other editors that are only free to read a pdf and have most features locked (your better off with simple pdf reader or just use browser). Features like OCR converting, direct text editing and everything you expect like combine/split/compress, marking, stamps, signature, convert, etc...

#### What's great

free tier (7)PDF conversion (3)document annotation (3)comprehensive PDF editing (4)PDF compression (1)signature support (2)OCR converting (1)

Helpful

Share

Report

10 views 2yr ago

Image 12: I EAT PEOPLE

I EAT PEOPLE

•1 review [...] Dr. Nellie Deutsch

•22 reviews

I love PDFgear for its comprehensive suite of features that make managing PDF documents effortless. Its user-friendly interface allows for seamless editing, annotating, converting, and signing of PDFs across multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, and iOS. The integration of AI-powered tools, such as the ChatGPT-driven co-pilot, enhances productivity by enabling natural language commands to perform tasks like summarizing documents or extracting key information. Additionally, PDFgear's commitment to providing these robust features for free, without the need for subscriptions or hidden fees, makes it an invaluable tool for both professionals and casual users. I use it with my students to review the work they do in their Moodle courses. [...] #### What's great

free tier (7)fast performance (1)easy setup (1)comprehensive PDF editing (4)

Helpful

Share

Report

39 views 9mo ago

Image 6: SouL RebeL

SouL RebeL

•7 reviews

This is hands down the best product for PDF to PPT, that I have used. Canva often lumps several elements together and jacks up the text. I (had) a desktop software that I was using that basically behaved the same way when uploading and editing, not enough detail, and slopping things together. I'm so happy with PDFgear, it saves me so much time now. I don't have to go through and redesign my designs. My only issue is that you all need to post a donation button so people you help can give back, seriously!

#### What's great

PDF conversion (3)

Helpful (1)

Share

results.content:
### Top forum threads

Gregory

#### What's great

ChatPDF is extremely useful when dealing with long or complex PDF files. I regularly use it to ask targeted questions, summarize key sections, and quickly locate specific information in research papers and contracts. The ability to get instant, context-aware answers without manually searching saves a significant amount of time. It’s especially valuable for students and professionals who need fast insights from dense documents.
